title: Servo Motor Sweep
subtitle: Rotate, Control, Learn!
overview: In this session, students will learn how a Servo Motor works and how to control its angle using Arduino UNO. Students will create a Servo Sweep project where the motor rotates from 0° to 180° and back continuously.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — servo setup, Arduino connection, students testing motor movement.

Difference between DC Motor and Servo Motor
Applications of Servo Motors in robotics and automation

activity.title: Servo Motor Sweep Project
steps: Connect Servo VCC wire to Arduino 5V
Connect Servo GND wire to Arduino GND
Connect Signal wire to Arduino Pin 9
Upload Servo Sweep code to Arduino
Observe the Servo rotating from 0° to 180°
Watch the Servo rotate back from 180° to 0°
Challenge: Change the speed of rotation using delay values

quiz.q: What is a Servo Motor mainly used for?
quiz.explanation: Servo Motors are used for precise angle and position control.

quiz.q: How many degrees does a standard Servo Motor usually rotate?
quiz.explanation: Most standard servo motors rotate from 0° to 180°.

quiz.q: Which Arduino pin is commonly used in the Servo Sweep example?
quiz.explanation: Servo motors are commonly connected to Pin 9 in beginner projects.

quiz.q: Which signal controls the Servo Motor angle?
quiz.explanation: Servo motors use PWM (Pulse Width Modulation) signals for angle control.

quiz.q: What happens in a Servo Sweep project?
options: Servo moves from 0° to 180° and back
quiz.explanation: In Servo Sweep, the servo rotates forward and backward repeatedly.

quiz.q: Which library is used for controlling Servo Motors in Arduino?
quiz.explanation: Servo.h library is used to control servo motors.

quiz.q: What does the Servo.write() function do?
quiz.explanation: Servo.write() sets the servo motor angle.

quiz.q: What is connected to the Servo signal pin?
quiz.explanation: The signal wire connects to an Arduino digital PWM pin.

quiz.q: Servo Motors are commonly used in:
quiz.explanation: Servo motors are widely used in robotics for controlled movement.

quiz.q: What will happen if delay time is increased in Servo Sweep code?
quiz.explanation: Increasing delay makes the servo rotate more slowly.
